definitions.target_outcome: A newly discovered LLM interface-safety bug confirmed by upstream contributors, not merely a local PromptABI finding.
confirmed_criteria: explicit project-member comment that the behavior is a bug

targets.selection_rationale: Active maintainers, frequent tool-call and reasoning-parser changes, existing tests for chat templates and tool parsers.
Active server with chat-template and tool-call parsing, prior accepted interface-boundary fixes (e.g. PR 18462).
Canonical source of chat templates and tokenizer configs; tokenizer/template changes frequently affect downstream interface contracts.

scanned_sources.excerpt:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project

from collections.abc import Sequence

from vllm.entrypoints.openai.engine.protocol import DeltaMessage
from vllm.reasoning.basic_parsers import BaseThinkingReasoningParser


class DeepSeekR1ReasoningParser(BaseThinkingReasoningParser):
    """
    Reasoning parser for DeepSeek R1 model.

    The DeepSeek R1 model uses <think>...</think> tokens to denote reasoning
    text. This parser extracts the reasoning content from the model output.
    """

    @property
    def start_token(self) -> str:
        """The token that starts reasoning content."""
        return "<think>"

    @property
    def end_token(self) -> str:
        """The token that ends reasoning content."""
        return "</think>"

    def extract_reasoning_streaming(
        self,
        previous_text: str,
        current_text: str,
        delta_text: str,
        previous_token_ids: Sequence[int],
        current_token_ids: Sequence[int],
        delta_token_ids: Sequence[int],
    ) -> DeltaMessage | None:
        ret = super().extract_reasoning_streaming(
            previous_text,
            current_text,
            delta_text,
            previous_token_ids,
            current_token_ids,
            delta_token_ids,
        )
        if (
            ret is not None
            and self.start_token_id not in previous_token_ids
            and self.start_token_id not in delta_token_ids
        ):
            if self.end_token_id in delta_token_ids:
                # end token in delta with more tokens,
                # extract reasoning content and content
                end_index = delta_text.find(self.end_token)
                reasoning = delta_text[:end_index]
                content = delta_text[end_index + len(self.end_token) :]
                return DeltaMessage(
                    reasoning=reasoning,
                    content=content if content else None,
                )
            elif self.end_token_id in previous_token_ids:
                # end token in previous, thinking content ends
                return DeltaMessage(content=delta_text)
            else:
                # no end token in previous or delta, reasoning content continues
                return DeltaMessage(reasoning=delta_text)

        return ret

candidates.contract_violation_claim: The DeepSeek-R1 streaming reasoning parser decides the reasoning/content transition from token-id membership rather than decoded marker text.
candidates.expected_behavior: A reasoning parser should detect the end-of-reasoning boundary robustly even if the marker is emitted as ordinary text tokens.
candidates.actual_behavior: extract_reasoning_streaming branches on start_token_id/end_token_id membership in *_token_ids.
candidates.triggerability_reason: <think> and </think> are atomic added special tokens for DeepSeek-R1, so the marker cannot be fragmented into ordinary text tokens; the id-only check is sound for this model.

duplicate_search.action: No upstream duplicate; candidate rejected on triage because the marker is atomic for this model family.

repro.runtime_requirement: CPU-only; no GPU needed for the parser path

disclosure.factual_impact: Hypothetical interface concern only; not triggerable for this model family, so no report is filed.

candidates.contract_violation_claim: The Hermes tool chat template renders message content adjacent to role delimiters and might allow role-boundary forgery.
candidates.expected_behavior: User-controlled content must not be able to forge assistant/system role headers or special tokens.
candidates.actual_behavior: Template uses macros and item-iteration constructs that exceed the supported analysis fragment.
candidates.triggerability_reason: PromptABI abstains: the template uses Jinja macros/filters outside the supported decidable fragment, so no soundness claim can be made.

duplicate_search.action: No claim filed; routed to the manual-review queue because automated analysis abstains.

disclosure.factual_impact: No finding; abstention recorded for auditability.

candidates.contract_violation_claim: Multiple XML-like function blocks can reuse a tool-call index and produce invalid streaming tool-call deltas.
candidates.expected_behavior: Each emitted function block should receive a distinct tool-call index/id in streaming deltas.
candidates.actual_behavior: Reduced PromptABI witness shows index reuse across consecutive function blocks (matches the existing upstream report).
candidates.triggerability_reason: Already demonstrated upstream; PromptABI reproduces it deterministically as a benchmark case.

queries: multiple function blocks tool call index
qwen xml tool parser index reuse
duplicate_search.action: Existing upstream issue 43713 covers this symptom; contribute the deterministic PromptABI-reduced regression test to that issue rather than opening a duplicate.

disclosure.factual_impact: Interface-contract violation in streaming tool-call deltas; already tracked upstream.

report.title: [tool-parser] Deterministic regression test for reused tool-call index across function blocks (Qwen XML)
report.summary: Expected: each streamed function block gets a distinct tool-call index. Actual: consecutive XML-like function blocks can reuse an index, producing invalid deltas. This is a deterministic regression test contributed to the existing upstream issue.
report.why_it_matters: Clients reconstruct tool calls by index; reuse corrupts multi-tool calls and breaks OpenAI-compatible streaming consumers.
report.actual_output: Two function blocks emit DeltaToolCall(index=0,...) twice.
report.proposed_fix: Allocate the tool-call index at function open rather than tool-call close.
report.promptabi_attribution: Reduced by PromptABI's parser-boundary analysis from the public reproduction; included to help maintainers, not as a severity claim.
